name: TED tenders - ingest every terminal Task dataset

nodes.name: Run persistent TED monitoring Task
nodes.notes: Verified with @apify/n8n-nodes-apify 0.6.10. Run task with Wait for Finish returns the terminal run object for SUCCEEDED, FAILED, TIMED-OUT, and ABORTED without applying the combined operation's success-only dataset gate.

nodes.name: Fetch terminal run dataset
nodes.notes: Fetches by the terminal run's defaultDatasetId for every status. This workflow is intentionally non-paginated: the retrieval limit is 1000 and the Actor appends exactly one summary control row, so the saved Task maxNewPerRun must be no greater than 999. Confirm that saved-Task cap during account-gated validation before activation. alwaysOutputData preserves the empty-dataset path.

nodes.name: Prepare every dataset record
nodes.notes: All dataset rows are persisted. Tender identity remains ted:<publicationNumber>; summary or malformed control rows use a run-and-row fallback key. A null tender title is preserved in payloadJson and normalized.title; displayTitle is only a presentation fallback.

nodes.name: Upsert committed TED records
nodes.notes: Create or select monitor-deliveries with the displayed columns. Upsert matches deliveryKey, so replaying ted:<publicationNumber> or a run-row fallback updates one row instead of inserting a duplicate.

parameters.content:
## Setup required
1. Install verified `@apify/n8n-nodes-apify` 0.6.10 or later.
2. Connect an Apify API-key credential.
3. Replace PASTEYOURTASKID with the persistent, primed Task ID.
4. Confirm saved Task maxNewPerRun is no greater than 999; retrieval is a fixed non-paginated 1000 because exactly one summary row is appended.
5. Create/select `monitor-deliveries` with the columns shown in **Upsert committed TED records**.

Every terminal run with a dataset is ingested before its status is reported. A null tender title is valid.
